description:
Advances: mutagen.json_schema, mutagen.coverage

Deferred from mutagen-wrd.26.

## Why this exists

mutagen-wrd.26 cached `Macro.to_string(r.original_ast)` and aliased the
result into both `mutation.results[].before` and `.before_source`,
fixing the duplicate-compute waste at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex
render_result/1. That's the v1 status quo minus the redundancy.

However, the AstCache module's @moduledoc (lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex)
documents `before_source` as a **verbatim source slice** —
byte-identical bytes from the originally-parsed source file taken by
`{line, column, end_line, end_column}` from AST node metadata, NOT a
re-serialisation via `Macro.to_string/1`. The two binaries are
byte-equal today but the contract differs (re-serialised vs. verbatim
slice).

mutagen-wrd.26 did not implement the verbatim slice path because:

1. Implementing it requires extending `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.Site`
   to carry `end_line` and `end_column` (currently only `line` and
   `column` are stored). That file is OUT OF SCOPE in mutagen-wrd.26's
   Allowed touches.
2. The enumerator's `walk_tree/6` already inspects `:closing` / token
   metadata in some forms; the extension is straightforward but a
   non-local change with its own test surface.

## Deliverable

- Extend `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.Site` with `end_line` and
  `end_column` fields (best-effort from AST metadata; `nil` when
  unavailable — see Out of Scope below).
- Thread the AstCache `source_text` into the runner result so the
  reporter can slice it.
- Update `render_result/1` (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex) to compute
  `before_source` as the verbatim source slice from `source_text` and
  `{line, column, end_line, end_column}` when end positions are
  available; fall back to `Macro.to_string(r.original_ast)` when
  metadata is missing (bare-literal sites + some macro-expanded
  forms).
- Update `.spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md` r4 + r12 to document the
  verbatim-slice behaviour AND keep the call-count cap from .26.
- Add tests that prove `before_source` is byte-identical to a
  hand-cut `{line, column, end_line, end_column}` slice of the
  original source for both 3-tuple and atomic-literal site shapes.

## Allowed touches
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (Site struct + walker
  end-position threading)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (carry source_text + end positions
  through to results)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (verbatim-slice rendering in render_result/1)
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md, mutation_enumeration.spec.md
- test/mutagen_ex/* (new + extended tests)
- Golden fixtures may need regeneration if before_source diverges
  from Macro.to_string output

## Out of Scope
- Bare-literal sites where the enumerator attributes the site to a
  PARENT operator's metadata (literal mutator's bare-value clauses);
  there's no enclosing AST node to slice from. The fallback
  Macro.to_string path remains for these.
- Multi-line spanning sites whose AST metadata does not carry a
  reliable end position (some macro-expanded forms).

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Test: 3-tuple AST site verbatim slice matches a hand-cut
      source slice (e.g. `a + b` from `lib/lane_fixture/arith.ex`)
- [ ] Test: bare-literal site falls back to Macro.to_string gracefully
- [ ] Test: Macro.to_string call-count cap from r12 still holds
- [ ] Golden fixtures regenerated and reviewed
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE
title: Verbatim source slice for mutation.results[].before_source
